88.reason: [Error: record should be set to the current operator (a[i]) before advancing i, not after parsing the next number. As written, record gets the first digit of the next number, causing incorrect evaluation of subsequent terms.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: record should be set to the current operator (a[i]) before advancing i, not after parsing the next number. As written, record gets the first digit of the next number, causing incorrect evaluation of subsequent terms.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  This line incorrectly assigns record to the first digit of the newly read number instead of the operator that was just processed, causing subsequent condition checks to treat a digit as an operator.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: 'i' currently points to the first digit of the newly parsed number, not the operator. 'record' incorrectly stores a digit character, causing subsequent addition/subtraction logic to fail.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.1 , reason:  [If trans_to_num didn't advance 'i', then a[i] may still be part of a digit, not an operator, causing record to be a digit character, leading to undefined behavior in later comparisons.]

20.reason: [This function incorrectly calculates the number by assuming all digits are processed in one go with decreasing powers of 10, but it doesn't update the index `i` in the caller, leading to reprocessing or skipping digits. Also, `p` is computed based on lookahead but then applied from the current position, which mismatches digit places.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This function incorrectly calculates the number by assuming all digits are processed in one go with decreasing powers of 10, but it doesn't update the index `i` in the caller, leading to reprocessing or skipping digits. Also, `p` is computed based on lookahead but then applied from the current position, which mismatches digit places.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.1 , reason:  [The logic for converting string to number is flawed. It assumes all digits are processed in one go and uses a power-of-10 multiplier that doesn't align with digit positions. For example, "123" would be calculated as 1*100 + 2*10 + 3*1 correctly only if p starts at 100, but the loop to compute p stops at j where a[j+1] is non-digit, so p ends up being 10^(length-1). However, the second loop increments i and divides p by 10 each time, which may work, BUT if called multiple times on overlapping segments (as in main), it will read beyond intended numbers because it doesn't stop at operator boundaries. Also, after reading a number, the caller doesn't update 'i' to the end of the number, leading to reprocessing same digits or skipping operators.]

27.reason: [This reads the first number starting at index 0, but trans_to_num does not return the new index, so 'i' remains 0. The subsequent while loop then scans from i=0 again, causing incorrect parsing.]
